name: Aloe Polysaccharide
description: Aloe describes a genus including over 500 species of flowering succulent plants that grow in the Southern peninsula and various islands. Aloe vera is the most common species of Aloe that is cultivated for agricultural and medical purposes. It is a perennial succulent xerophyte with elongated leaves that contain a clear gel. The aloe vera gel is a rich source of polysaccharides, vitamins, minerals, amino acids, anthraquinones, enzymes, lignin, saponins, and salicylic acids. Some of the polysaccharides that are active constituents of aloe vera gel include acemannan, mannose, and cellulosic glucose. While aloe vera has a long history of commercial uses, it is still widely used in cosmetic, food and pharmaceutical products.
